exemplar.title: How do Ninh and O'Brien explore the impacts of trauma in The Sorrow of War and The Things They Carried?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do Ninh and O'Brien explore the impacts of trauma in The Sorrow of War and The Things They Carried?

subject.title: English Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Literature focuses exclusively on the study of literature, developing students' appreciation and critical analysis of literary works from various cultures, periods, and genres. Students examine poetry, prose, and drama through close reading, comparative analysis, and exploration of literary techniques and themes. The course cultivates sophisticated interpretive skills and deep understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience across different cultural contexts.

strengths: The title and opening paragraph clearly establish and maintain the focus on trauma representation across both texts.
The research question is sharply stated and consistently linked to each analytical section.
Methodology is comprehensive, drawing on a wide selection of relevant primary and secondary sources with clear evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: A small amount of content drifts briefly into psychological commentary (endorphins, addiction) rather than strictly literary analysis.
A few citation lapses (misplaced footnote numbers and incomplete page references) marginally reduce methodological polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although two strands were placed at band-5 due to minor drifts and citation lapses, the overall communication is accurate, purposeful, and consistently tied to the research question. The methodology is broad and well executed, with robust evidence of effective source selection. These qualities meet the top-band descriptors for Focus and Method, warranting the maximum score of 6.

strengths: Quotations from both primary texts are aptly chosen, contextualized, and meaningfully interpreted, demonstrating deep textual knowledge.
Secondary scholarship (Chen, Silbergleid, van der Kolk, etc.) is woven into the argument to support nuanced readings.
Terminology such as âanaphora,â âembedded narrative,â and âdissociationâ is used with precision and consistency.
weaknesses: A minor factual slip occurs when The Things They Carried is described partially as a novel rather than a story collection.
Occasional typographical errors appear in technical terms (e.g., âfree and direct discourseâ).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates excellent, coherent understanding of both texts and effectively applies a wide range of relevant sources. Subject-specific terminology and traumaâstudies concepts are accurately and consistently integrated. The minor factual slip and typos do not undermine the overall excellence, justifying the top mark of 6.

strengths: Research is broad and generally wellâfocused on the guiding question, with most sources directly informing the comparative analysis.
Sustained comparative analysis of narrative fragmentation, metatextuality, and dissociation is well supported by close readings and critical voices.
Argument structure is clear, and links between literary technique and thematic implications are explicit and compelling.
weaknesses: Evaluation tends to affirm secondary scholarship rather than pursue rigorous counterâarguments or alternative interpretations.
The discussion of biochemical endorphins feels tangential and would benefit from tighter linkage to literary technique.
Critical evaluation remains good but stops short of the consistently probing critique needed for full topâband marks.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Critical Thinking fall solidly within the 7â9 band. The essay offers strong, wellâsupported analysis and a coherent argument, though it lacks sustained critical evaluation and counterâargument development. These qualities justify a high band score of 9 out of 12.

strengths: The overall structure (introduction, thematic sections, conclusion, bibliography) is clearly appropriate and aids readability.
Section headings effectively signpost the argument and help the reader navigate the comparative analysis.
weaknesses: The table of contents lacks accurate page numbers, hindering quick navigation.
Footnote formatting and numbering are inconsistent in places, creating minor distractions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all core requirements with a clear structure and generally correct layout. Minor lapses in the table of contents and footnote styling prevent the award of the top mark, placing the work firmly in the 3-point range out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student demonstrates a clear literary-critical method: close textual analysis supported by secondary criticism.  A wide selection of critics is consulted (Chen, Vernon, Silbergleid, etc.) and integrated, and MLA-style references plus a complete bibliography are supplied.  Citations are generally accurate, though a few footnote numbers are misplaced and page-reference conventions occasionally lapse.  Because these lapses slightly reduce effectiveness, the work meets the upper-band descriptors but merits the lower score in that band.

Required layout elements are mostly met: title, word count, section headings and an alphabetised bibliography are present; in-text citations and footnotes follow a recognisable system.  A few technical lapses (uneven footnote numbering, occasional spacing errors in URLs) prevent flawless application, but the overall formatting is sound and aligns with the top bandâs descriptors, warranting 3 rather than 4.

The bibliography demonstrates breadth and relevance: contemporary literary criticism, psychological scholarship, and authoritative editions of the primary texts are employed.  Each secondary source directly informs interpretation rather than being decorative, and quotations are generally well integrated.  Because some references (e.g., Hampton on endorphins) are tangential to literary purpose and a few page citations are incomplete, the overall research quality is good but not uniformly excellent, placing it at the bottom of the 7-9 band.

The research focus â trauma in two Vietnam-war texts â is declared in the title and sharpened by the explicit question âHow do Ninh and OâBrien explore the impacts of traumaâ¦?â.  From the opening paragraph onward the student keeps this frame in view, repeatedly signalling that the essay will compare stylistic and thematic treatment of trauma.  Key terms such as âstylistic and authorial methodsâ, âmetatextualityâ and âfragmented narrativeâ make the purpose transparent.  The clarity, concision, and consistent reference to the guiding idea show communication that is both accurate and purposeful, matching the 5-6 band description.

An argument is clearly structured around five thematic lenses, and each section ends by relating findings to the research question.  The student evaluates authorial choices (e.g., the âhappening-truthâ vs. âstory-truthâ distinction) and recognises limitations in conveying trauma.  Evaluation, however, tends to affirm rather than rigorously critique secondary scholarship, and counter-arguments or alternative interpretations are rarely pursued in depth.  This places discussion firmly in the good range, earning a 7.

The essay offers sustained comparative analysis: the student juxtaposes narrative fragmentation, dissociation, and metatextuality across both works and explains how each device conveys trauma.  Interpretive claims are usually supported with close reading and critical voices, and links between technique and theme are explicit.  Some passages drift toward paraphrase or descriptive summary, and a few analytical leaps (e.g., biochemical comments on endorphins) are only lightly evidenced, so the depth and effectiveness are strong but not consistently sophisticated, meriting an 8 within the 7-9 band.

The essay follows EE conventions: abstracted introduction, signposted thematic sections, conclusion, and full bibliography.  Headings aid navigation and footnotes are present.  Minor issues (a partially formatted contents table, some footnote clutter and inconsistent superscript styling) indicate that while the structure is clearly appropriate, it is not polished enough for full marks in this band, so 3 is awarded.

Quotations are aptly selected, contextualised and interpreted, showing strong knowledge of both primary texts.  Secondary sources are not merely name-checked: ideas from Chen on ambiguity, Silbergleid on metafiction and van der Kolk on trauma theory are meaningfully woven into the argument, indicating coherent understanding.  Minor factual slips (e.g., describing The Things They Carried as a short-story âcollectionâ yet sometimes treating it as a novel) prevent the knowledge from being uniformly excellent, placing the strand at 5 in the 5-6 band.

Subject terminology such as âanaphoraâ, âsemantic fieldâ, âembedded narrativeâ, âfree indirect discourseâ and âmotifâ is used accurately and usually applied to precise textual moments.  Concepts from trauma studies (dissociation, liminality) are also integrated correctly.  Occasional typographical errors in terms (e.g., âfree and direct discourseâ) and a few vague labels keep the usage shy of total consistency, so a solid 5 is appropriate.

The research question is stated verbatim and is tightly focused on a single literary concern (the representation of trauma) in two well-defined primary texts.  Throughout the essay each section is explicitly linked back to this focus (e.g., headings on âFragmented narrative structureâ, âMetatextualityâ).  A very small amount of material (brief references to endorphins, addiction) drifts toward psychology rather than literary analysis, so absolute precision is not maintained at every point; hence the strand fits comfortably but not perfectly within the top band â a 5 rather than a 6.

annotations.comment: Some footnotes include HTML artifacts, obscuring citation clarity; remove stray tags and verify consistency.
The analysis adeptly applies terminologies like âanaphoraâ and âpresent tenseâ to elucidate how narrative voice conveys trauma.
This descriptive passage drifts into paraphrase; consider integrating deeper interpretive claims to support your thesis.
The title clearly states the investigative focus on trauma recovery across both texts, establishing clear purpose and scope.
The research question is explicitly stated and sharply focused, guiding the subsequent analysis effectively.
The inclusion of a word count demonstrates compliance with formatting requirements.
The table of contents outlines main sections but lacks page numbers and consistent structure, hindering navigation.
Headings in the contents are clear, but ensure each is followed by an accurate page reference for reader guidance.
The introduction provides strong contextual knowledge and aligns the two texts for comparative analysis.
Citation of secondary trauma sources indicates methodological breadth, but the MatÃ© reference is cut off and needs completion.
Footnotes are used, but ensure consistent formatting of author-date or numerical styles throughout the document.
This transitional sentence reiterates the essayâs evaluative aim, reinforcing focus on stylistic and authorial methods.
Section headings like âFragmented narrative structureâ effectively signpost arguments, aiding clarity and organization.
Footnote numbering resets irregularly; adopt a sequential numbering system to maintain bibliographic precision.
Dissociation section provides a coherent thematic development, linking methods across both texts effectively.
Parenthetical references occasionally use bracketed math tags; standardize to MLA in-text citation style for consistency.
Consider beginning each section with a sentence that explicitly ties back to your research question to reinforce focus.
The extended comparative account of dissociative narrative layers demonstrates thorough cross-textual analysis.
In-text citation includes a math tag around page numbers; correct this to improve readability and adherence to style.
The âWomen as amuletsâ metaphor insightfully unpacks the symbolic role of Phuong, highlighting nuanced thematic parallels.
Endorphin discussion feels tangential to literary focus; tighten relevance by linking psychological commentary directly to textual technique.
Footnote displays HTML escape codes (&lt;sup>), indicating OCR errors; correct these to ensure accurate references.
The rape imagery analysis effectively employs antonymic adjectives to illustrate traumaâs transformative impact.
Expand on how the movement from innocence to brutality in imagery aligns with broader themes of memory and loss.
Secondary source citation lacks full publication details; provide complete bibliographic information in the bibliography.
